lastReleaseMessage: v0.10.0: bar-raise drain (D-006 loopback bind, D-007 import/rollback strict backup-fail, error_log + logEvent, daily prune, regex hoist, analytics surface modules, dedup guard, dashboard preservation, drift-fix F1-F8); .finances/ removed from git index (D-001/D-002 enforcement)

barRaise.topFinding: The loop finally turned: for the first time across four audits the DESIGN/DECISIONS/CHANGELOG drift (F1-F11) is applied rather than re-catalogued, but it sits uncommitted in the working tree and never went through release.ps1, so GitHub and the dashboard still show the old drift. The same weekly pass surfaced a real security item: Nick's bank statements remain in git history (commits 44b7b10, f670d17, 43ea16b) and would leak the moment this private repo is shared per D-002's distribution model. The chronic root, seven handler-named skills not installed, is unchanged: the drift only drained because a session hand-ran the executor, not because the loop can self-close.
